title: [BUG] use excel.stream.xlsx.WorkbookReader Chinese garbled characters appear？？
author: danielmlc

comments.author: ligolas
comments.body:
@danielmlc It seems that I encountered the same situation (not 100% sure that we are in the same situation). And after some dig in I managed to find the cause：
 In `lib/utils/parse-sax.js` buffer chunk(size 16384 in my case determined  by  `zlib`  trunkSize default value  )  is decoded  to string by bufferToString in `lib/utils/browser-buffer-decode.js` inside which the decode is accomplished by `TextDecoder` which cannot properly handle the utf8 multi bytes characters being cut off into two buffer situation. 
In node, `string_decoder` can be used to replace TextDecoder:

> When a Buffer instance is written to the StringDecoder instance, an internal buffer is used to ensure that the decoded string does not contain any incomplete multibyte characters. These are held in the buffer until the next call to stringDecoder.write() or until stringDecoder.end() is called.

But in browser, maybe we should try to implement the  mechanism above ourself

comments.author: danielmlc
ligolas
comments.body: @danielmlc I'm sorry , that's because I made a mistake in the code I committed and pushed (which is not the version I've tested),  the decoder must be de declared outside bufferToString to make use of the inner buffer logic. I'm very sorry

comments.author: ligolas
comments.body: BTW,  Someone have provided a more elegant [way](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/issues/2084) which actually do the exact same thing (but use internal logic, not explicitly). Both way should work out  the same result.

comments.author: danielmlc
vicary
comments.body:
The method @ligolas mentioned solves the issue in a really clean way, I added this one line of code in `workbook-reader.js` and it worked.

```ts
entry.setEncoding("utf-8");
```
